prompt:
Anatomical muscle-activation diagram of the "滑雪跨步" (ski steps / lateral alternating jumps) exercise, front view, facing the viewer. Person posture: Athletic person mid-air during a lateral ski jump: airborne with both feet together to one side (currently jumping leftward), both knees slightly bent in mid-flight, body angled to the left like a downhill skier; arms held forward and slightly to the right for counterbalance, like ski poles; torso slightly forward-leaning; plain black athletic shorts and gray tank top. Body rendered with semi-transparent skin so underlying muscles are visible. Highlight in saturated red the primary working muscles: quadriceps, gluteus maximus, and gluteus medius of both legs for lateral propulsion. Highlight in orange the secondary stabilizers: calf muscles and core muscles for stabilization. Other muscles in pale neutral gray. Add small clean labels with thin black arrow leader lines pointing from each label to its target muscle group. The labels MUST be in simplified Chinese characters using a clean sans-serif font. Use exactly the following labels: "股四头肌 / 臀大肌" pointing to the quadriceps and gluteus maximus; "臀中肌" pointing to the gluteus medius for lateral motion; "小腿 / 核心" pointing to the calf and core area. 

CRITICAL CONSTRAINTS — these are non-negotiable:
1. ALL TEXT IN THE IMAGE MUST BE SIMPLIFIED CHINESE CHARACTERS ONLY.
2. ABSOLUTELY NO ENGLISH WORDS, NO LATIN LETTERS, NO PINYIN ANYWHERE IN THE IMAGE.
3. NO romanization or transliteration of muscle names.
4. NO captions, NO subtitles, NO step-by-step text overlays.
5. ONLY the muscle labels listed above should appear as text.

Style: clean medical textbook illustration, off-white background, no facial features (head shape may be shown but no eyes/nose/mouth), anatomically accurate proportions, no shadows on background, no watermarks, no signature.
